title: ws: GET_KEY_PACKAGE and GET_HPKE_KEY accept arbitrary pub_id without format validation
description: Both GET_KEY_PACKAGE (ws.rs line 956) and GET_HPKE_KEY (line 1096) accept a params.pub_id from the caller and pass it directly to store queries without validating it is 64 lowercase hex chars. WHISPER, SEALED_WHISPER, and GROUP_ADD all validate their target pub_id with the 64-char hex check before the DB call. GET_KEY_PACKAGE and GET_HPKE_KEY are inconsistent: an attacker can pass a multi-kilobyte string as pub_id, injecting an arbitrarily-sized string into every DB query that touches the key_packages or hpke_keys tables. The DB queries are parameterized so SQL injection is not possible, but the missing check allows resource waste (large string allocation per DB round-trip) and fingerprinting (timing oracle: a 64-char valid pub_id that exists returns Some, one that does not returns None, but an invalid format is indistinguishable from a not-found result). Fix: add the same len==64 + all lowercase hex check present in WHISPER before the store call, and return INVALID_PARAMS if invalid.
